texts.full_text: s directly proportional to the surface tension, density and inversely related to its molecular weight. The aim was also study various rheological properties of zinc oxide eugenol impression pastes manufactured by Indian companies. It will help dental students as well clinical practitioners in choosing a particular brand for their use.\[[@ref5]\] Materials And Methods {#sec1-2} ====================== Ten brands were selected randomly from the list and evaluated with respect to rheological properties (flowability, viscosity) alongwith surface tension of impression pastes. The manufacturers\' name was concealed in order not to influence any subjective bias. Flow measurement: A standard stainless steel cylindrical cup 50 mm diameter ×128mm height having a smooth inner wall is used for measuring the flow property \[Figure [3](#Fg6){ref-type="fig"}\]. The paste samples were prepared by mixing equal amounts of powder and liquid in glass dish. Each impression material was mixed according to manufacturer\'s instructions, using ZOE spatula (S.S., 10 cm ×5 mm). After the pastes reached a consistency that it could be easily poured from cup without any sagging or running off at room temperature (\~26°C), they were used for testing. The flowability was measured in millimeter as per ISO standard.\[[@ref3]\] Ten measurements of each sample (n = 10) and ten repetitions on the same day, with a minimum interval time between samples being five minutes. The average values from all readings are reported \[[Table](#Tg6){ref-type="table"}\]. Viscosity measurement: The viscosity was measured using ZOE viscometer. It consists of two stainless steel plates (25 mm diameter × 3mm thickness) which have a smooth and polished surface, separated by the distance between them in such way that they do not touch each other \[[Figure](#Fg7){ref-type="fig"}\]. The paste samples were prepared as described above. A small amount of material was placed on one plate with spatula (S.S., 10 cm ×5 mm) and the plates are brought into contact, such that a layer thickness between them is equal to half millimeter \[[Figure](#Fg7){ref-type="fig"}\]. The viscosity measurement unit has an electronic timer which measures time in seconds for material flowing fro

texts.full_text: s with capitals decorated with animal heads. The first capital depicts a ram’s head, the second shows two lion heads and on the third is placed an angel head. The west portal has five archivolts and is even richer in decoration than the northern one. Finally the south portal has three archivolts, each resting on shafts with capitals decorated with foliage, animal and human faces. In addition to this there are relief panels above the outer arch of the portal which show a monk and his staff, a bishop holding an orb and cross, and a king holding an orb. Interior The church has retained its medieval frescoes from two different periods, one in the beginning of the 13th century and the other from the late 15th century. Federal Minister for Minorities in Pakistan states that he still had no confirmation about the incident, but if the Taliban had had actually done this, they tarnished the image of Islam. He said according to the media reports, Sikhs had been forced to pay 20 million rupees as Jazia. Community Action Centre Chariman Rev. mario Varano states, "We will provide settlement assistance for displaced refugees and we commend the initiative of SAHRA." SAHRA urges and encourages the Government of Canada to take stern action and a stand on this issue, since Canada has always been a leader in the promotion and implementation of human rights around the world.

The identified interactions between huntingtin and specific transcriptional regulators suggest that these regulatory mechanisms play a crucial role in the pathogenesis of HD. By targeting these dysregulated processes with novel compounds, it may be possible to mitigate or even reverse some aspects of neuronal damage observed in this neurodegenerative disorder The efficacy demonstrated by medications acting on transcriptional regulators both and particularly notable improvements seen within HD mouse models highlights their potential for clinical application. The District is a political subdivision of the State of Texas, with a legislative mandate to conserve, preserve, and protect the groundwater resources, including springflows, of the aquifers within its jurisdictional area. The District regulates groundwater within its jurisdiction by adopting, implementing, and enforcing regulations and management programs that address water demand, springflow protection, aquifer recharge, and other management strategies. The purpose of issuing the proposed permit is to allow for the ongoing use of the waters of the Aquifer by end-users under the management authority of the District while conserving listed species and the ecosystems upon which they depend. Adoption of a multispecies habitat conservation approach, rather than a species-by-species or project-by-project approach, will reduce the costs of implementing minimization and mitigation measures for the covered species; and eliminate cost and time-consuming efforts associated with processing individual incidental take permits for each user of the Aquifer. In addition, the multispecies habitat conservation approach provides a program including avoidance, [[Page 12523]] minimization, and mitigation for each species that is coordinated on a landscape level that provides increased benefits to the covered species. Bibliography Site Internet Comité 21 Perspectives Two generations of local Agenda 21 More than 900 French local authorities have committed in a local Agenda 21 for the 1990s. Half of them were engaged before the Grenelle process in 2007 and have already finished their first action plan. Today, two generations – and maybe three - of local Agenda 21 coexist in France: The Agenda 21 “first generation”, elaborated from a diagnosis and definition of the priorities by the elected people. They are embodied in an action plan characterized by the dominancy of environmental issues and the ambition of eco-responsibility of the administration. They concern the authorities which committed before Grenelle process, but also those who did it between 2010 et 2014 ; The Agenda “second generation”, developed after a phase where citizens were involved in local projects and actions through participative democracy processes (like the Agenda of the City). They are characterized by their focus on social issues. The ambition is to create an inclusive society, taking into account environmental challenges but also economic ones like employment or housing policies; The “third generation” which would be defined as a synthesis between first two generations with both ambitions: eco-responsibility and inclusion of all actors (elected people AND citizens). In the use of a solid state component or device, the presence of high power levels results in high temperature operation which causes distortions in the operating characteristics of the device. texts.full_text: a color developer under thermal activation to produce a visible color change. Upon heating, the leuco dye undergoes oxidation facilitated by the electron-accepting compound, resulting in a colored form of the dye. This process is generally stable and results in an irreversible coloration. In contrast, the invention discussed here introduces a significant advancement through its reversible thermosensitive recording medium. This innovation allows for the repeated formation and erasure of colored images on the medium by modulating the applied thermal energy. The key feature of this reversible medium is its use of specific electron-donating coloring compounds and electron-accepting compounds that enable the coloration reaction to be reversed. The reversible process involves two stages: the writing stage and the erasing stage. During the writing stage, when heat is applied, the electron-donating coloring compound reacts with the electron-accepting compound, forming a colored image on the medium. This colored state is stable under normal conditions but can be reverted to the original colorless state by applying a different thermal treatment, constituting the erasing stage. This reversible capability offers substantial benefits for applications requiring temporary markings or multiple uses of the same recording medium. For example, in environments where information needs to be updated frequently without replacing the medium, such as in dynamic labeling systems or educational tools, this technology provides an eco-friendly and cost-effective solution. Moreover, the ability to control the thermal energy applied to the medium allows for precise management of the coloration and decoloration processes. This precision can enhance the reliability and performance of devices employing this reversible thermosensitive recording medium. In summary, the present invention enhances the functionality of thermosensitive recording mediums by introducing reversibility into the color formation process, thereby expanding their potential applications in various fields requiring dynamic,

texts.full_text: the role of bacteriophages and integrons in the evolution of pathogenic bacteria]. The role of bacterPhages are viruses that infect bacterial cells. They play an important part in the process known as Horizontal Gene Transfer (HGT), which involves the movement of genetic material between organisms other than by traditional reproduction, or vertical transfer from parent to offspring. Bacteriophages can contribute to HGT through transduction, where viral genes move into a bacterium and integrate with its genome via integrases encoded within their own genomes. This process is particularly significant in bacterial evolution as it allows for the rapid acquisition of new traits that may provide advantages such as antibiotic resistance or virulence factors which make some bacteria pathogenic (disease-causing). Integrons are a key player facilitating HGt among prokaryotes like Bacteria and Archaea by capturing mobile gene cassettes from various sources, including phages themselves. и scroll'е ЗдравСтвуйте. Есть маленький код, который устанавливает значение top у блока в зависимости от положения scroll'а. Проблема в том, что не во всех браузерах оно плавно отображается, т.е. при scroll'е происходят резкие скачки (браузера: IE, Opera <11 и т.д.). Как убрать эти скачки и сделать полностью плавные движения? Код: window.onscroll = function() { document.getElementsByTagName('div')[0].style.marginTop = window.pageYOffset + 'px'; }; body {height: 2000px; } div { width: 200px; height: 200px; background-color: black; }

texts.full_text: se two masters. Both of those serve the content. Note that I didn't say that you had to have the content mapped...but I doubt you can name a documentary where the content wasn't significantly mapped out. Maybe it was an outline...but I can't think of a documentary that didn't have a whole heck of a lot more than that before it began. Funny that you mention that outlines, even scripts, compromise objectivity. If there was such a thing (and I don't believe there is), I think the compromise begins with the camera. You make a choice to point in one direction rather than another, a time to turn it on and turn it off. These at the very least influence content, but since the dawn of documentary production, most of us have argued that these are ethical choices as well as artistic ones. I'll go further and say that there's no more inherently subjective and actively ethical artform than the documentary. Discuss. Maybe not here though. :-) Do we need to open a production philosophy community? I'm enjoying this. Anyway, let's forget objectivity. Maybe it's a goal, maybe it isn't. But there's still some kind of goal fpr the content. It may or may not be the biggest goal of the PRODUCTION -- although I absolutely believe it's always the PRIMARY goal of any documentary production -- but it's one of them. Again, I may be being overly reductive, but if you don't have an actual story to tell, there's no point for clients to spend money, for investors to invest, no point in taking the camera out of the bag. These transitions and absences ...of transitions are used to represent the digital data stored on the CD disk. The compact disc and CD player operate by digitally sampling the information or music source, and recording the sampled signal as a series of pits and lands of variable lengths formed on a polycarbonate plastic substrate coated with an aluminum reflective coating and protected by a layer of clear lacquer. A land is the surface of the substrate between the pits. Usually this digitally sampled information is represented by the transitions between the pits and lands or vice versa, and by the absence of transitions. These transitions and absences of transitions are used to represent the digital data stored on the CD disk. The pit-to-land transition represents a binary digit (bit) in the digital data, with the presence of a pit indicating a logical "1" and the absence of a pit indicating a logical "0". The length of each pit or land is proportional to the magnitude of the digital signal being represented. This allows for high fidelity reproduction of music by accurately capturing the nuances of the original audio signal. The damper system, which is the subject of this invention, is designed to improve the stability and accuracy of the CD player's laser beam as it reads the pits and lands on the disk. texts.full_text: ning aloft, from balloons and zeppelins to the Space Station Search Main menu Skip to primary content Skip to secondary content Home Posts Events Schedule Reviews of my Book About Richard Foss Monthly Archives: December 2014 High Flying Advertising – Saga of the Space Pizza Posted on December 31, 2014 by hrfoss For at least 150 years savvy food producers have recognized the advantages of having famous people be seen eating or drinking your brand; Victorian cricketers, famous actors, and even Pope Leo XIII all endorsed various products. (In the case of the pope, it was “Mariani Wine”, a mixture of alcohol and cocaine. He wasn’t the only celebrity endorsement for this powerful mix of stimulants and depressants- Thomas Edison and Ulysses S. Grant also allowed the company to use their likenesses in advertising.) Astronauts were instant celebrities who could get various endorsements, but it took until 2001 before a food product was advertised by an astronaut on board the International Space Station (ISS). The story of space pizza began with Pizza Hut's marketing team looking for innovative ways to promote their brand. They approached NASA and proposed sending one of their pizzas into orbit as part of a promotional campaign. The idea faced numerous challenges, including technical issues related to food safety in microgravity environments and the need to ensure that it would not interfere with astronauts' health or mission objectives. After extensive negotiations and modifications to meet space agency standards, Pizza Hut sponsored an experiment where Russian cosmonaut Yuri Gidzenko received a small pizza on board the ISS. This high-flying advertising stunt was more than just sending food into orbit; it represented significant advancements in understanding how foods behave outside Earth's atmosphere. It also highlighted new possibilities for marketing and brand exposure through space exploration, setting precedents that could influence future commercial activiTies beyond our planet’s surface. The event garnered considerable media attention worldwide, demonstrating the power of aligning a consumer product with cutting-edge scientific endeavors like human spaceflight. A gas in which there are free charged carriers (electrons) is called ionized or partially-ionized if it contains only small concentrations thereof and fullyionized otherwise; however all such gases may be referred as plasmas. A completely neutral particle-free environment cannot exist since any electrically-charged object will generate an electrical field that, in turn, generates a plasma. The term “plasma” is often used to describe the gas phase of partially-ionized or fullyionized media which are generated by applying energy from one source (e.g., electromagnetic radiation) and/or another. In this context plasmas may be classified as thermal if they have Maxwellian velocity distributions, i.e., when all particle species within a plasma environment reach an equilibrium state with respect to each other; otherwise the particles will not possess such velocities. What is Wolfram Language?

The Wolfram Language is a highly powerful, flexible and intuitive computational language designed to provide both speed and ease of use. It is built on more than two decades of cutting-edge mathematics, research and development by Wolfram Research.

Installing and Launching the Wolfram Language

To start using the Wolfram Language, you first need to install it on your computer. The installation process is relatively straightforward and can be done following the instructions provided in the official documentation. Once installed, you can launch the Wolfram Language by double-clicking on the executable file or through the start menu.

Learning the Wolfram Language

The Wolfram Language comes with a comprehensive documentation and tutorials to help you learn and master its features. Additionally, there are numerous online resources, including video tutorials, books and community forums, where you can get assistance and share your knowledge with other users.

texts.full_text: either of the mutant CD44 isoforms, CD44HDelta67 or CD44EDelta67, did not exhibit the capacity to assemble pericellular matrices or to bind and internalize the fl-HA. Cotransfection of CD44Delta67 mutants together with CD44H reduced the size of the HA-dependent pericellular matrices. Transfection of bovine articular chondrocytes with CD44Delta67 also inhibited pericellular matrix assembly. Collectively, these results indicate an obligatory requirement for the CD44 receptor cytoplasmic domain for ligand (HA) binding, formation and retention of the pericellular matrix, as well as CD44-mediated endocytosis of Ha.
